//: Configuration for pymarkdownlnt — the markdown linter run by `make lint`.
We disable rules that fight against mkdocs Material conventions or our
deliberate docs-site style. Anything not listed here keeps the default.
Rationale per disabled rule is below. Enable any of these by removing
the entry; the default is `enabled: true`.

line-length.//: Tables for env vars / API fields naturally exceed 80 chars. Code blocks too.

no-inline-html.//: Material admonitions and theme partials emit/accept HTML in markdown.

no-bare-urls.//: We sometimes write bare URLs inside admonitions or table cells.

first-line-heading.//: Tutorial pages start with YAML frontmatter (`---`), not an H1.

no-duplicate-heading.//: Per-crate API skeleton pages legitimately repeat 'Quick links', 'Modules' across crates.

no-trailing-punctuation.//: Some headings are sentences ending in '?' or '.' (e.g. FAQ-style).

no-emphasis-as-heading.//: We use bold spans (e.g. `**filename.toml**`) as inline labels above code blocks — pymarkdown reads these as 'should be a heading', they shouldn't.

code-block-style.//: We use both fenced and indented code in different contexts intentionally.

list-indent.//: Material admonitions accept either 2- or 4-space indented list items.
